mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean or Rank IC > 0.05 in 80% of samples despite high FEDFUNDS levels.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances when UNRATE crosses the 0.2pp monthly threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and output, which directly boosts corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases yield negative 20-day SPX returns in > 70% of observations during early-cycle expansion regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Higher total nonfarm payrolls indicate robust employment growth, which sustains consumer spending and corporate earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS levels show no statistically significant correlation with 5-day forward returns (p > 0.10) across all tested samples.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 320.0, persistent inflation forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing borrowing costs and suppressing equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL remains above 320.0.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly personal consumption expenditures reveals underlying demand resilience; when both series trend upward together, it confirms sustainable economic expansion and drives positive 20-day equity returns.
failure_condition: Co-movement of GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to generate a positive Rank IC over 20-day horizons in > 50% of rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates above 4.0% signal a restrictive monetary regime that aggressively caps liquidity and discount rates, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are non-negative in > 55% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0% during tightening cycles.
